reviews.review_description: I initially paid €13.99 for this application and it didn’t have any subscription, which is why I paid for it because I’ve already had a subscription with Nord VPN for $50 a year. I could’ve easily side loaded this on to my Quest but since this was a one time payment offer, I decided to pay for this recently as the last month and today I go to use it and I see that they’re talking about a subscription now. I don’t do subscriptions anymore because everybody wants a subscription and I don’t work to pay soft handed programmers to create mediocre services to charge me a subscription for. This is disgusting and I’m gonna reach out to Meta because I need compensation for this. ￼

great little app to begin with, then it tries to make you pay £9.99 a month.
Yeah Nah, maybe £35 a year would be fine. Like VPNs for pc.
So one star but reduce the price and it will quickly become 5 star

This is very dishonest in my opinion, my app that I bought is essentially stolen and the worst thing about it is nobody cares. This app has no support I can see, meta is not interested at all so nothing I can do. I notice reviews are turning into 1 star so thanks to meta for giving us that option at least. Not all though, which makes me wonder if the remaining reviews are even real, how can they be

You don’t need this. I bought a tiny vpn router from Amazon installed the free proton vpn and connected my headset to its hotspot. For £27 I now have vpn forever which is what I paid this company for before they took it back. Shame there is no zero star option
I purchase this and used it then dev added you need the pay monthly after I already bought the app?
Again, like everyone has stated. I paid for the app and they decided to change to a subscription.

Even if I got it for free, it is a law that says that there needs to be some indication that there was gonna be a subscription service AS LONG as the service is paid, so not only are you not giving adequate information, you're also breaking the law.

No soup for you

Same as everyone else. I bought this app for £11. no where did it say that a subscription would also be needed. I used it for about a week and with a sudden update it's now requesting a subscription that's way over the odds. This is criminal. If it was free then fine I'd just uninstall it. But the fact that I paid and there was no warning until after they had my money is proper low. If I'd known I wouldn't have brought it. I feel completely robbed. I'm going to push to get my money back and everyone else should as well. disgrace.

I originally paid for this outright. It’s slow and full of bugs but it sometimes did the job and I paid a one time fee so I didn’t mind. Now after paying a few months later they demand a monthly fee for a slow buggy VPN. No thanks. Total con. Didn’t even give a refund on the one time fee. DONT USE UNLESS YOU WANT SLOW, laggy VPN that only sometimes works

reviews.review_title: Purchased but now have to subscribe
reviews.review_description:
This was great when i purchased a few weeks ago (around £12). Now they have changed this to a subscription model… surely they can’t do that to those who paid for the app to start with??? Shame because it did work well.
UPDATE - i have checked and they legally cannot retroactively convert your existing purchase into a subscription without your explicit consent.
They can change the app’s model for new users (e.g., future downloads become subscription-based), but your purchase should remain valid under the original terms you paid for.
In the UK/EU, this would also fall under Consumer Rights Act 2015, which protects you from unfair changes to digital purchases.

This is what i have found online

Because SpatialVPN is a VPN app, and Meta’s policy states that VPN apps require special permission, this adds complexity: the developer must comply with Meta’s platform rules (which may involve what kind of monetisation or fees are allowed).

If the developer tries to force a subscription on you (for the same app and version) without giving you the option to keep what you bought, you might argue it is a breach of what you purchased — especially under consumer-rights law (in UK/EU).

For some reason, despite already paying for this app and it working fine they've now made it impossible to use without a paid subscription!

Don't buy it, do yourself a favour and just use the free extensions that are now available on the quest browser for VPN.

When I first got this app when it was free, it was great.I could dupe items in animal company freely and fast.But then it started to cost money. Then it became a whole dang subscription. And the app doesn’t even work. I’m truly disappointed because this was my favorite duping app.

I am extremely disappointed and honestly angry with this app. When I purchased it, i thought its “one-time fee” – no hidden charges, no subscriptions. I paid the full amount upfront believing I was getting a lifetime product.

Now suddenly they’ve introduced a subscription plan, and the features I already paid for are being locked behind monthly payments. This feels nothing less than a scam. If they were planning to charge a subscription later, then why did they take a one-time payment at all? What was the point of calling it “one-time fee” just to trap users?

This is dishonest, misleading, and completely unfair to paying customers.
I strongly advise everyone to avoid this app unless you like getting tricked into paying twice for the same thing.

Explain how this isn't theft.  Maybe the company should continue services as agreed to when people bought at a flat rate before change to sub model. Pissed off.  F these guys.  0 stars.

The app that was purchased for $15 is now requiring a $100 a year subscription.  As the reviews show...Many are feeling scammed, and rightfully so.  Spatial, do the right thing and grandfather those that paid for the app or refund our money.
